title: Disposing AnimationController leaks
desc: Every AnimationController must be disposed in the State.dispose() method, or the ticker keeps running after the widget is removed from the tree.
descAr: كل AnimationController يجب التخلص منه في State.dispose()، وإلا سيستمر المؤقت بعد إزالة الـ widget من الشجرة.
summary: Forgetting to call controller.dispose() in State.dispose() leaves orphaned tickers running in the background, wasting CPU and leaking memory. Every AnimationController must be manually disposed.
problem: Missing dispose() on an AnimationController means the underlying Ticker continues firing after the widget is gone. This causes a 'Ticker disposed' error or silent CPU usage in the background. With 8 animation controllers on a single page, forgetting one means 7% constant CPU even after the user navigates away. The error only surfaces when the ticker eventually overflows, making it hard to debug.
problemTitle: Orphaned tickers keep running after widget disposal
solution: Override dispose() in any State that owns an AnimationController and call controller.dispose(). For multiple controllers, call dispose() on each. Using AutomaticKeepAliveClientMixin does not exempt you — you must still manually dispose. The pattern: declare, init in initState, dispose in dispose. Never skip the third step.

callout.text: Key idea: the Dart analyzer with close_sinks lint catches this. Enable cancel_subscriptions and close_sinks in analysis_options.yaml to catch leaks early.

baDesc: The code diff is adding a single method override, but the runtime behaviour change is significant.

before.desc: Orphaned ticker runs in background. CPU at 12% after navigation.
before.code: class _MyState extends State { late AnimationController _ctrl; @override void initState() { super.initState(); _ctrl = AnimationController(vsync: this, duration: 300ms); } // dispose() is missing — leak! }

after.desc: Controller disposed. Zero background CPU. Memory stable.
after.code: class _MyState extends State { late AnimationController _ctrl; @override void initState() { super.initState(); _ctrl = AnimationController(vsync: this, duration: 300ms); } @override void dispose() { _ctrl.dispose(); super.dispose(); } }
